title: Telegram HTML 400 Bad Request: malformed entity crashes message delivery
source: Ash live report 2026-06-22 â€” Pi error: 'A request to the Telegram API was unsuccessful. Error code: 400. Description: Bad Request: can\'t parse entities: Can\'t find end of the entity starting at byte offset 1415'
current_state:
_format_for_telegram converts Pi markdown to Telegram HTML. It handles **bold** â†’ , *italic* â†’ , `code` â†’ , fenced ``` â†’ 
, [text](url) â†’ . Known failure modes: (1) DOTALL regex on **bold** can produce ...spanning newlines... that Telegram rejects if the span crosses certain Unicode boundaries. (2) If the LLM outputs an unclosed ** or unmatched *, the regex produces a dangling open tag. (3) Content split across 4096-char chunk boundaries may cut inside an HTML tag. _reply_chunks has a fallback: if HTML parse_mode fails, retry with parse_mode=None. BUT: parse_mode=None on raw markdown text (with *, _, [, etc.) can also fail with Telegram's default parser or produce garbled output.
target_state: On Telegram 400 error in _reply_chunks: strip ALL HTML tags from the chunk (re.sub('<[^>]+>', '', chunk)) and resend as plain text with parse_mode=None. This guarantees delivery even when formatting is broken. Additionally, add a pre-send validation step in _format_for_telegram: count open vs closed <b>, <i>, <code>, <pre> tags and close any unclosed ones before returning. This catches the most common unclosed-tag case.
migration_plan: 1. _reply_chunks fallback: change the except clause from `bot.send_message(chat_id, chunk, parse_mode=None)` to `bot.send_message(chat_id, re.sub('<[^>]+>', '', chunk), parse_mode=None)` â€” strip all HTML before sending as plain text.

2. _format_for_telegram: after building the result string, add a tag-balance check. For each of <b>, <i>, <code>, <pre>: count opens and closes; if opens > closes, append the missing close tags. This is O(n) and cheap.
3. Chunk boundary safety: in _chunk_text, do not cut inside an HTML tag â€” scan backwards from the chunk boundary to find a safe split point (after a >, before a <).

4. Tests: test_telegram_formatting.py (already exists) â€” add cases: (a) unclosed **bold produces valid closed ...; (b) HTML-with-malformed-tag falls back to stripped plain text; (c) chunk boundary does not split inside a tag.
risk_notes: The HTML stripper (re.sub('<[^>]+>', '', text)) is the nuclear option for the fallback â€” it removes all formatting. That is acceptable: a readable plain-text message is strictly better than a 400 error that shows nothing. The pre-send tag balancer handles the common case without stripping formatting at all.
